titleEn: The Network of Reading Universities at the USAL
excerptEn: 27/10/2010 | IUCE On Thursday 4 November 2010 at 12 noon, the presentation of the Network of Reading Universities at the University of Salamanca will take place in the Faculty of Translation and Documentation, with the collaboration of the IUCE. Taking part in the presentation will be: – José Ángel Domínguez Pérez, Vice-Rector for Teaching. –…
contentEn:
27/10/2010 | IUCE

On Thursday 4 November 2010 at 12 noon, the presentation of the Network of Reading Universities at the University of Salamanca will take place in the Faculty of Translation and Documentation, with the collaboration of the IUCE.

Taking part in the presentation will be:

– José Ángel Domínguez Pérez, Vice-Rector for Teaching.

– Eloy Martos Núñez, President of the Network of Reading Universities.

– Carlos Fortea, Dean of the Faculty of Translation and Documentation.

– José Antonio Cordón García, Director of the USAL's Master's Degree in Publishing and Representative of the Network at the USAL.

– Raquel Gómez Díaz, Representative of the Network at the USAL.

– Mª José Rodríguez Conde, Director of the IUCE at the USAL.

This will be followed by the lecture “Digital publications: between intellectual property and free access to information”, delivered by Fernando Carbajo, a lecturer at the University of Salamanca.

excerptEn: 22/10/2010 | IUCE On 9, 10 and 11 March 2011, Cádiz will host the EVALtrends 2011 International Conference, organised by the EVALfor Research Group of the University of Cádiz, with which members of the IUCE collaborate on various projects. Focusing on the topic Assessment for learning at…
contentEn:
22/10/2010 | IUCE

On 9, 10 and 11 March 2011, Cádiz will host the EVALtrends 2011 International Conference, organised by the EVALfor Research Group of the University of Cádiz, with which members of the IUCE collaborate on various projects.

Focusing on the topic Assessment for learning at university: Innovative experiences, EVALtrends 2011 aims to be a forum for debate offering the opportunity to contribute, share and disseminate innovative experiences and practices that put forward useful and viable alternatives in the assessment of competences and their learning outcomes at the University.

This activity has the collaboration of the GRIAL Research Group in Interaction and e-Learning of the University of Salamanca, as well as the presence among the members of the scientific committee of Professor Mª José Rodríguez Conde, Director of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), and of Professor María Esperanza Herrera García, Dean of the Faculty of Education of that university.

At the IUCE, we encourage all lecturers at the University of Salamanca to take part in this event.

titleEn: The 2010 PDI Training Programme comes to an end
excerptEn: This past September we completed the 2010 edition of the University Teaching Staff Training Programme. Since April this year, the IUCE has run a total of 72 activities in which more than 500 hours of training have been delivered. In this way, a total…
contentEn:
This past September we completed the 2010 edition of the University Teaching Staff Training Programme.

Since April this year, the IUCE has run a total of 72 activities in which more than 500 hours of training have been delivered. In this way, a total of 1,298 places were allocated, covering applications from more than 700 lecturers at the University of Salamanca.

A highlight of this edition was its aim of familiarising teaching staff with the teaching methodologies and the student assessment process entailed by the European Higher Education Area, with particular emphasis on the use of the Studium virtual platform, the handling of the interactive digital whiteboard and the use of digital audiovisual technology in teaching practice.

As an important part of this programme, we would like to highlight the Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff, which aims to equip new lecturers with the competences they need to begin their careers as university teaching professionals.

Pictured are some of the students who successfully completed the Initial Training Programme for University Teaching Staff (FIPU) in this edition, together with Mª José Rodríguez Conde, Director of the Institute.

titleEn: Honorary Doctorate for Germán Sánchez Ruipérez
excerptEn: 28/09/2010 | USAL Communications Office. On the afternoon of Tuesday 28 September 2010, the Assembly of Doctors of the University of Salamanca approved the appointment of the three new honorary doctors: Eikichi Hayashiya, whose candidature was presented by Ovidi Carbonell (Department of Translation and Interpreting and Department of…
contentEn:
28/09/2010 | USAL Communications Office. On the afternoon of Tuesday 28 September 2010, the Assembly of Doctors of the University of Salamanca approved the appointment of the three new honorary doctors: Eikichi Hayashiya, whose candidature was presented by Ovidi Carbonell (Department of Translation and Interpreting and Department of Sociology and Communication); Germán Sánchez Ruipérez, defended by Mª José Rodríguez Conde (University Institute of Education Sciences); and Stephen Whitaker, backed by Miguel Ángel Galán Serrano (Department of Chemical and Textile Engineering).

The votes cast by the 85 Assembly members present at the session, plus the 17 delegated votes, were as follows:

Germán Sánchez Ruipérez 93 votes in favour 3 votes against 5 blank votes
Stephen Whitaker 85 votes in favour 1 vote against 15 blank votes
Eikichi Hayashiya 79 votes in favour 3 votes against 19 blank votes

– The Assembly of Doctors of the University of Salamanca approves the appointment of three new honorary doctors.

– Green light for the honorary doctorate for Germán Sánchez Ruipérez.

– The University approves the appointment of Ruipérez, Whitaker and Hayashiya as honorary doctors.

titleEn: Lecture and Presentation of the Book by José Ortega Esteban
excerptEn: On Wednesday 16 June 2010 at 12 noon, the presentation of the book “Delincuencia, Reformatorio y Educación Liberadora” by José Ortega Esteban took place in Room 12 (Angelitos) at the IUCE. Taking part in the presentation were: – Dr José Ortega Esteban, Professor of Social Pedagogy at the University of Salamanca and author of the book.…
contentEn:
On Wednesday 16 June 2010 at 12 noon, the presentation of the book “Delincuencia, Reformatorio y Educación Liberadora” by José Ortega Esteban took place in Room 12 (Angelitos) at the IUCE.

Taking part in the presentation were:

– Dr José Ortega Esteban, Professor of Social Pedagogy at the University of Salamanca and author of the book.

– Dr Jaume Trilla Bernet, Professor of Theory and History of Education at the Autonomous University of Barcelona and author of the book's prologue.

– Dr María José Rodríguez Conde, Director of the IUCE, University of Salamanca.

Afterwards, Dr Jaume Trilla Bernet gave a lecture on “Narrative pedagogies in university teaching, the case of José Ortega Esteban's book, Delincuencia, reformatorio y educación liberadora”

We took the opportunity of the book presentation to pay a small tribute to José Ortega Esteban for his 10 years of dedication and leadership of the IUCE and for the achievement of having made it a Research Institute.

The IUCE, together with the Vice-Rector for Teaching of the University of Salamanca, presents the PDI Teaching Training Programme (leaflet in pdf) in its 2010 edition, on 15 April at 9.30 am, at the Rectorate (Portrait Room), before the press.

Pre-registration for the courses opens on Tuesday 20 April at 12 noon, via this link.

titleEn: 1st IUCE Award for technologies for teaching innovation
excerptEn: The University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca, in collaboration with the IncuProject Foundation of Madrid, is launching the first call for the 2010 IUCE Award for technologies for teaching innovation. Aim The 2010 IUCE Award for technologies for teaching innovation aims to encourage teaching staff to implement…
contentEn:
The University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca, in collaboration with the IncuProject Foundation of Madrid, is launching the first call for the 2010 IUCE Award for technologies for teaching innovation.

Aim

The 2010 IUCE Award for technologies for teaching innovation aims to encourage teaching staff to implement technological solutions that support students' education. The award is also intended to recognise the work of lecturers who are concerned with and interested in incorporating educational technologies into their teaching.

Summary of the rules:

Eligibility: lecturers at Spanish universities, individually or as a team.

Prize: 2,000 euros and an accrediting diploma.

Subject matter: The entries submitted will deal with projects applying ICT (Information and Communication Technologies) to teaching innovation, as detailed in the rules.

Deadline and how to submit entries: The deadline for sending a .zip file (max. 9MB) with the two required documents (a sworn declaration that the work has not previously received an award or been published, and the report on the experience) is 24 June at 2 pm. It is very important to pay attention to the formats specified in the rules because, following the University of Salamanca's recommendations on the use of open standards and formats, documents will not be accepted if they do not comply with those specified in the rules.

Rules of the call (pdf) || Web form for submitting the entry
